stories.title: Commissioner Allam Highlights Mental Health Funding as a Budget Priority
stories.excerpt: Vice Chair Nida Allam identified mental health services and the county's co-responder crisis program as essential priorities for the FY2026-27 budget at the BOCC's April budget work sessions. Public comment on the budget is open now and the hearing is May 26 at 7 PM at Commissioners Chambers.

stories.title: Commissioner Allam Backs Affordable Housing Bond for November Ballot
stories.excerpt: Vice Chair Nida Allam is supporting efforts to place an affordable housing bond on Durham County's November ballot. The proposed bond would fund housing for residents earning below 60% of area median income. The Board of Commissioners continues reviewing the proposal.

stories.title: FY2026-27 Budget Public Input Period Is Open
stories.excerpt: Durham County is accepting public comment on the proposed FY2026-27 budget. Submit written comments online or attend the public hearing on May 26 at 7 PM at Commissioners Chambers, 200 E. Main St. County Manager Claudia Hager presents the recommended budget on May 11.

stories.title: Durham Public Schools Submits FY2026-27 Budget Request to County
stories.excerpt: The DPS Board of Education formally submitted its FY2026-27 budget request to the county, seeking increased funding to cover staffing, facilities, and mental health services across 53 schools.

stories.title: FY2026-27 Budget Schedule: Key Dates for Public Participation
stories.excerpt: County Manager Claudia Hager presents the recommended FY2026-27 budget on May 11 at 7 PM. The public hearing is May 26 at 7 PM at Commissioners Chambers, 200 E. Main St. The Board of Commissioners votes on adoption June 8.

stories.title: Planning Commission Reviews Major Southside Rezoning Proposal
stories.excerpt: The City-County Planning Commission will consider a rezoning application in the Southside neighborhood at its April 23 meeting. The proposal would allow higher-density residential development. See the Planning Commission agenda for the full application and public comment information.

stories.title: BOCC Takes Up Affordable Housing Funding at April Meeting
stories.excerpt: The Board of Commissioners discussed affordable housing funding options at its April 14 regular meeting, including a potential bond measure. See the official meeting video and agenda for details on actions taken.

stories.title: GoTriangle Pursuing Federal Funding for Durham–Chapel Hill BRT
stories.excerpt: GoTriangle continues work on the Durham–Chapel Hill Bus Rapid Transit corridor along NC-54. The agency is seeking federal Small Starts funding to advance the project. See gotriangle.org for current project status.

stories.title: Durham County Expands Co-Responder Crisis Intervention Program
stories.excerpt: Durham County is expanding its co-responder crisis intervention program, which pairs mental health clinicians with law enforcement for behavioral health calls. The program is rolling out countywide starting May 1.

stories.title: Ellerbe Creek Greenway Extension Breaks Ground
stories.excerpt: Durham Parks & Recreation broke ground on an Ellerbe Creek Greenway extension, adding new trail miles in northeast Durham. See the Parks & Recreation website for the latest project timeline.

stories.title: Durham County Seeks Applicants for Housing Advisory Board
stories.excerpt: Durham County is looking for residents to serve on its Housing Advisory Board, which advises commissioners on affordable housing policy. Applications are due May 1 at dconc.gov.

stories.title: Free Vaccination Clinic at Durham Human Services on June 9
stories.excerpt: The Durham County Health Department is hosting a free vaccination clinic on June 9 from 10 AM to 2 PM at 414 E. Main St. Flu, COVID-19, and standard adult vaccines will be available. No appointment needed.
